goal: Build a gravity-feed station: 4 cubes spawn at z=1.0 above the conveyor, fall onto the belt, then are picked into a bin. Tests handler robustness with cubes that have non-zero initial velocity from gravity drop. Industrial pattern: hopper-fed conveyor with robotic singulation.
thoughts:
Six gravity-feed specifics for CP-21 (extends CP-01 — same scene + bin, different cube spawn):
1) Cubes spawn at z=1.0 (~17cm above belt top z=0.83) → fall ~13cm under gravity (v ~ 1.6 m/s at impact). Stick-slip + rubber material catches them on belt.
2) sleepThreshold=0 critical: settling-from-fall puts cubes momentarily into low-velocity-but-not-zero state. PhysX would normally sleep them; sleepThreshold=0 keeps physics ticking.
3) Spaced 0.30m apart along conveyor x — gives belt time to carry them sequentially into pick zone (vs all 4 falling simultaneously which causes pile-up).
4) Robot picks cubes that arrive at sensor zone. Sensor at x=0.4 gates pick; cubes that haven't reached x=0.4 keep belt-riding.
5) drop_target = Bin bbox center (no special targeting). Cubes go to single Bin.
6) Settle_state restores spawn z=1.0 (NOT belt-rest z=0.83) — cubes will re-fall on canonical re-entry, demonstrating the gravity feed each time.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 4 cubes at z=1.0 (above belt) — they'll fall on canonical play
for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -1.05, -0.7, -0.35]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 1.0], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Bin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-21 inherits CP-01 mechanically but spawns cubes 17cm above belt — they fall and land before pick begins. Tests handler's tolerance for cubes with non-zero initial velocity. settle_state restores fall height so each canonical re-entry demonstrates the gravity feed. Future: pair with create_gravity_dispenser tool when that's built (Tier C).
failure_modes: Cubes fall too high → bounce too much before settling. z=1.0 is ~17cm; lower would settle faster but reduces 'feed' visualization.
All cubes spawned simultaneously at same x → land in same spot, pile up. Stagger by x=[-1.4, -1.05, -0.7, -0.35] (0.35m apart).
Cube_4 lands within reach (x=-0.35, |x|<0.7) — controller picks immediately. Belt-feed visual reduced for it.
Without sleepThreshold=0 → cubes settle, PhysX sleeps them, then belt's friction-based velocity skips sleeping bodies → cubes don't move with belt. Same as CP-01.
